151.reason: The line counter is incremented here after skipping a single-line comment. However, the main loop will also encounter the '\n' character at the end of the comment (or the next character if the comment ends at EOF without newline) and increment 'line' again in the next iteration. This causes the line number to be off by one for subsequent tokens. In the specific test case, this logic error might not directly cause the observed output difference if there are no comments, but it is a logical bug. More importantly, looking at the expected vs buggy output, the buggy output has `INTCON 0` instead of `INTCON 10` for `num/10`. Let's re-examine the division logic.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The line counter is incremented here after skipping a single-line comment. However, the main loop will also encounter the '\n' character at the end of the comment (or the next character if the comment ends at EOF without newline) and increment 'line' again in the next iteration. This causes the line number to be off by one for subsequent tokens. In the specific test case, this logic error might not directly cause the observed output difference if there are no comments, but it is a logical bug. More importantly, looking at the expected vs buggy output, the buggy output has `INTCON 0` instead of `INTCON 10` for `num/10`. Let's re-examine the division logic.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop exits when it encounters '\n' or reaches the end of the file. If it exits due to '\n', the main for-loop will increment 'line' again in the next iteration, causing the line number to be incremented twice for a single newline. If it exits due to EOF, incrementing line is also incorrect. This leads to incorrect line numbers for tokens following comments.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [After skipping a single-line comment ending with '\n', the loop will increment 'i' again at the end of the for-loop, causing the '\n' to be skipped without updating 'line' a second time. However, the real issue is in the 'else' branch below: when handling a single '/', the code does 'i++' at the start, but if it's not followed by '/' or '*', it should NOT have incremented 'i' before deciding it's a DIV token. This causes the next character to be skipped incorrectly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [After skipping a single-line comment ending with '\n', the loop will increment 'i' again at the end of the for-loop, causing the '\n' to be skipped without updating 'line' a second time. However, this is not the main issue here. The real problem is in the 'else' branch below: when handling '/', if it's not followed by '/' or '*', the code correctly treats it as DIV, but fails to decrement 'i' to compensate for the initial 'i++', causing the next character to be skipped. This leads to missing tokens or incorrect parsing.]

167.reason: [This line is missing! After incrementing 'i' at the beginning of the block, if the next character is not '/' or '*', we are processing a single '/', but the for-loop will do i++ again, so we must decrement i here to compensate. Without this, the character after '/' is skipped. In the test case, "num=num/10" becomes "num=num/0" because '1' is skipped.]
